---

### 📧 Hardcoded Email Addresses

All 3 were found in **`src/UsersController.scala`**, within what appears to be a hardcoded in-memory user list (lines 9–11):

| Line | User | Email |
|------|------|-------|
| 9 | Alice | `alice@example.com` |
| 10 | Bob | `bob@example.com` |
| 11 | Charlie | `charlie@example.com` |

---

### ⚠️ Notes
- All three emails are **hardcoded directly into `User(...)` constructor calls**, suggesting a static/mock dataset is being used instead of a database or external data source.
- While these particular addresses use the safe `@example.com` domain (commonly used for testing/demos), hardcoding user data like this is a bad practice — it should be replaced with a proper data store (e.g., a database or seeded fixtures file) to keep the code flexible and secure.
